title: Next gameplay phase: activate the inland route and make the back half of the world feel authored
summary: Twenty-fourth structured packet for Eco Explorer. Packet `023` is now fully complete: the field-season board is live, route-aligned coastal content landed, treeline has a traversal shelter proof, a third small station utility exists, and inland revisit process moments are visible. The next leverage is to activate the inland half of the world as a real second chapter. That means one second live field-season route, alpine route content fuel, one short tundra traversal proof, one compact route-completion acknowledgment, and only then subtle inland corridor-authored beats.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the inland route beat sheet and pacing for the second live line on the field-season board.
execution_order.why_now: The second route should feel authored and chapter-like, not improvised from existing route fragments.

execution_order.goal: Activate the second live inland route on the field-season board.
execution_order.why_now: The back half of the world now has enough traversal and process support to become a real second chapter.

execution_order.goal: Review the second route for pacing, clarity, and whether the inland half now feels authored instead of merely unlocked.
execution_order.why_now: The route shell should be critiqued before more alpine content and traversal are shaped around it.

execution_order.goal: Add alpine route content fuel across forest, treeline, and tundra.
execution_order.why_now: The inland route needs its own discoveries, comparisons, and notes instead of borrowing all of its momentum from the coastal board work.

execution_order.goal: Review the alpine route content pass for science quality, pacing, and whether it distinguishes the inland chapter from the coastal one.
execution_order.why_now: The route should feel richer, not just colder and more crowded.

execution_order.goal: Add one short tundra traversal proof aligned to the inland route.
execution_order.why_now: A compact high-latitude movement proof will make the inland route feel like a true place, not just a route board checkbox.

execution_order.goal: Review the tundra traversal proof for readability, tone, and kid-friendly difficulty.
execution_order.why_now: Tundra is easy to over-harden or over-flatten if it is not critiqued directly.

execution_order.goal: Add one compact route-completion summary or atlas acknowledgment at the field station.
execution_order.why_now: Two routes are enough to justify a small sense of seasonal progress without turning the station into a dashboard.

execution_order.goal: Review the route-completion acknowledgment for tone, readability, and whether it stays fieldwork-first rather than score-like.
execution_order.why_now: Recognition is good leverage, but it can easily become gamified in the wrong way.

execution_order.goal: Add quiet authored inland corridor beats after the second route and route-completion shell are stable.
execution_order.why_now: The inland seams will teach better once the route layer gives players a reason to walk them deliberately.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
Do not respond to a clear queue with disconnected side features.
The second route should make the inland branch feel authored, not just unlocked.
Keep one live inland route at a time instead of opening several equal-weight route tracks immediately.
The field-station board should stay compact and notebook-like.
Tundra traversal should stay short, readable, and shelter-aware.
Route-completion acknowledgment should feel like fieldwork recognition, not a score screen.
Corridor-authored route beats should stay quiet and note-backed rather than becoming tutorial chatter.
acceptance_focus: The queue again points at one coherent gameplay phase instead of going blank.
The inland half of the world becomes a true second chapter rather than a late-game afterthought.
Later corridor and atlas work now have a stronger authored inland route to build on.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-field-season-routes-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-act-two-route-matrix-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-treeline-shelter-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-route-reward-ethics-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-inland-route-phase.md, and this packet. Treat the clear queue as a signal to make the inland half of the world feel authored. The next live move is a second field-season route, not another disconnected feature drop.
